rulings.Case Title: ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION INFORMATION CENTER 'EPIC' et al VS. CALIFORNIA STATE BOARD OF FORESTRY AND FIRE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Motion For Judgment On The Consolidated Petitions For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 1, 2009, line 4, PETITIONER ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION INFORMATION CENTER Joint Motion For Judgment On The Consolidated Petitions For Writ Of Mandate. Motion is granted. Petitioner's claims are ripe for adjudication. Petitioners present a pure legal issue (facial challenge) that is not dependent upon a set of hypothetical facts. Respondents adopted regulations in excess of their statutory authorities. Respondent DFG's "Incidental Take Permit Guidelines for Timber Operations 2007" sections 787.1(a)(1), 782.2, 782.2, 787.4(a), 787.5(a) & (b), 787.6(a), and 787.9 violate the fair argument standard. ITP Guideline 787.9(d) suffers from the same infirmity that was identified in Communities for a Better Environment vs. California Resources Agency (2002) 103 Cal.App. 4th 98. The regulation also violates CEQA's Mandatory Findings of Significance and the CESA duty to consider jeopardy. Respondent BOF's "Coho Salmon Incidental Take Assistance 2007" exceeds the authority of BOF by adopting regulations that purport to determine minimization and full mitigation under CESA (Fish & Games Code section 2081), where BOF has no authority to make a determination of what constitutes minimization and full mitigation under CESA. Accordingly, sections 916.9.2 [936.9.2], and 923.9.2 [943.9.2] are struck down. The Court does not address the comment issue. This action is not time-barred. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN ENORME et al VS. M-JSF INVESTMENTS LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 1, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT M-JSF INVESTMENTS LLC MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Moot in view of ruling on Demurrer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN ENORME et al VS. M-JSF INVESTMENTS LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 1, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT M-JSF INVESTMENTS LLC DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiffs fail to allege facts showing they were tenants in January 2006 when defendant invoked the Ellis Act. No facts pled showing that the claims are not time barred. Delayed Discovery Rule not applicable because plaintiffs had constructive notice of any alleged violation of the Rent Ordinances when defendant filed its notice of intent invoking the Ellis Act on January 31, 2006. No reasonble probability that defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARCO A MARCELLA VS. HSI PRODUCTIONS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 1, 2009, line 16, DEFENDANT HSI PRODUCTIONS, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to Strike is granted with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege facts that show defendant had either advance knowledge of the unfitness of an employee and employed him with a conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others, or authorized or ratified the wrongful conduct for which the damages are awarded, or was personally guilty of oppression, fraud, or malice. The Complaint filed to allege any oppression, fraud or malice as required by Civil Code section 3294(b). (CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARCO A MARCELLA VS. HSI PRODUCTIONS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 1, 2009, line 16, DEFENDANT HSI PRODUCTIONS, INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege facts to support a reasonable expectation of privacy while on a public street. Plaintiff must plead specific facts that defendant's employees intended to cause harmful or offensive conduct or that he believed he was about to be touched in a harmful or offensive manner, or that he was threatened to be touched in a harmful or offensive manner. (See CACI 1301 and 1320.)=(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARK A. OLSON et al VS. ADI SIMAYOF et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 1, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT SIMAYOF INC. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion to strike granted without leave to amend. CC section 1717.5 provides "If there is a written agreement between the parties signed by the person to be charged, the fees provided by this section may not be imposed unless that agreement contains a statement that the prevailing party in any action between the parties is entitled to the fees provided by this section." =(302/CWW)
